prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: At the bottom of the bright, rippling sea, where sunlight poured through the water in warm, dancing stripes, lived a little octopus named Ollie. Ollie had eight soft arms that loved to curl around seashells and count shiny pebbles, but he was very shy. When colorful fish zipped past or playful dolphins clicked hello, Ollie's skin would blush a shy shade of purple and he would tuck himself behind a coral, hoping no one would notice. He watched the others play and listened to their laughter like it was a secret treasure, and more than anything he wished he had friends to splash with and share seaweed sandwiches—if only he knew how to say hello.
